text.title: Trello Kanban Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Trello kanban board must enforce. These are HARD rules — convenience does not override them.

rule.statement: Board has 5-7 lists. WIP limit encoded in list name ("In Progress (WIP: 3)"). Trello does not block moves — Butler rule or human review enforces the limit.
rule.rationale: More than 7 lists creates cognitive overhead; named WIP makes violations visible without a paid Power-Up.

rule.statement: ≤12 labels in a fixed taxonomy: type (Feature, Bug, Tech Debt, Docs), priority (P0-P3), status modifier (Blocked, Quick Win). One type label per card; new labels go through a PR-like change request.
rule.rationale: Label bloat breaks filters; agents inventing labels destroy board hygiene fast.

rule.statement: Story points, sprint, component live in custom fields (queryable via API). Encoding "[5pt] Add OAuth" in the title is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Custom fields are queryable + filterable; title strings are not. Agents that parse titles for data are fragile.

rule.statement: Done cards are archived (closed=true) at sprint end, never deleted. Archived cards remain accessible via the API for velocity reconstruction.
rule.rationale: Deleted cards cannot be retrieved; archived ones are first-class for historical reporting.

rule.statement: Agents cache board / list / label IDs once per session in memory or in constitution.md. Writes use IDs, never name-to-ID lookups on every call.
rule.rationale: Name lookups waste rate-limit budget; cached IDs cut request count and survive name changes.

rule.statement: API writes capped at 100 requests / 10s / token. Bulk operations batch sequentially with backoff; parallel agents must coordinate. 429 responses MUST be handled explicitly.
rule.rationale: Unhandled 429 = silent data loss; the documented limit is non-negotiable.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
